title: Pre-commit hook: auto-format staged TS with biome (auto-fix, non-blocking)
description:
Add a git pre-commit hook that auto-formats staged TypeScript/JS/JSON/CSS with biome and re-stages the fixes — it should FIX things for you, not block the commit. No failing/aborting on formatting; just apply and continue.

Project already has the tooling: biome.json at repo root and scripts in package.json (lint = 'bunx biome check .', lint:fix = 'bunx biome check --write .'). Just no hook runner yet.

Use lefthook (same as the evee project — reference /Users/calum/code/github.com/0x63616c/evee/lefthook.yml):
1. Add lefthook as a devDependency (bun add -d lefthook) and an 'prepare'/postinstall to run 'lefthook install' so the hook is wired on clone.
2. Create lefthook.yml at repo root with a pre-commit format command:
   pre-commit:
     parallel: true
     commands:
       format:
         glob: '*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,json,jsonc,css}'
         run: bunx biome check --write --no-errors-on-unmatched {staged_files}
         stage_fixed: true
   - stage_fixed: true re-stages biome's fixes so the commit includes them.
   - --write auto-fixes; --no-errors-on-unmatched keeps it from erroring when no files match.
3. IMPORTANT per Calum: the format step must NOT block the commit. Use biome safe auto-fixes only (do NOT add --error-on-warnings; do NOT put typecheck/lint-as-gate in pre-commit). evee's lefthook also runs a blocking typecheck — do NOT copy that part; this hook is format-only and must always let the commit through.
4. bun-first: all commands use bunx/bun, never npm/npx.

Optional follow-up (not this ticket): a separate non-blocking commit-msg or pre-push for heavier checks; keep CI as the real gate.
acceptance_criteria: Committing a badly-formatted .ts file auto-formats it and includes the fixes in that same commit, without aborting. lefthook.yml exists at repo root; lefthook installed via prepare script; uses bunx biome check --write --no-errors-on-unmatched {staged_files} with stage_fixed: true. The hook never blocks a commit on formatting/lint. No npm/npx. Verified: stage a misformatted file, commit, confirm it lands formatted in one commit.

title: Next12Hours: remove fake placeholder temps + shimmer; inherit global retry
description:
apps/web/src/components/tiles/Next12Hours.tsx + apps/api/src/services/weather-service.ts.
1. Fake temps (P0): Next12Hours.tsx:7-20 PLACEHOLDER (12 hardcoded hours 74°→63°) + :45 data ?? PLACEHOLDER — full fake bar chart on failure behind a small 'cached' badge. API PLACEHOLDER_HOURLY weather-service.ts:97-110 returned on any error (:171). Remove both; service throws; tile shows shimmer when !data.
2. retry:2 override at Next12Hours.tsx:41 — remove so it inherits the global QueryClient infinite-retry config.
Build on shared Skeleton.
acceptance_criteria: Open-Meteo outage: shimmer skeleton, no fake bars, no 74/76/78 values. No PLACEHOLDER/PLACEHOLDER_HOURLY constants. retry:2 override removed (inherits global retry).

title: EventsTile: remove fake placeholder events + shimmer; speed up refetch
description:
apps/web/src/components/tiles/EventsTile.tsx + apps/api/src/services/events-service.ts.
1. Fake events (P0): EventsTile.tsx:5-9 PLACEHOLDER ['Gorgon City','Chris Lake','Florida 2026'] + :104 (isLoading||isError||!data ? PLACEHOLDER : data) — fake concerts on load AND error. API PLACEHOLDER_EVENTS events-service.ts:40-45 returned on empty DB (:55) and DB error (:63-66) with frozen day counts (now stale). Service throws on DB error; tile shows shimmer on isLoading/isError.
2. Source gap: events are manually seeded (db/seed.ts:8-29), no calendar integration. Flag only — do NOT require building a calendar integration here. At minimum DB errors must propagate.
3. Refetch too slow: POLL.events = 30min (lib/hooks.ts:26, EventsTile.tsx:100). Drop to <=5min so real events recover quickly after an outage.
Build on shared Skeleton.
acceptance_criteria: DB error/empty: shimmer skeleton, no 'Gorgon City'/'Chris Lake'/invented day counts. No PLACEHOLDER constants. POLL.events <=5min. DB errors surface as tRPC errors, not 200-with-fake-data.

title: ClimateTile: remove fake FALLBACK + shimmer, show live HVAC action, drop extra icon box
description:
apps/web/src/components/tiles/ClimateTile.tsx + apps/api/src/services/climate-service.ts.
1. Fake FALLBACK (P0): ClimateTile.tsx:26 { target:70, ambient:72, mode:'auto', action:'Idle' }, :36 data ?? FALLBACK — shows 70°F/72° on first mount + outage; no isLoading/isError branch exists. API FALLBACK climate-service.ts:13-18,33,59; mutation hardcodes ambient:72 in trpc/routers/climate.ts:29,40-41. Service throws; tile shows shimmer when !data; remove hardcoded mutation ambient.
2. Live HVAC action discarded: API returns action ('Cooling'|'Heating'|'Idle') from hvac_action (climate-service.ts:52-55) but ClimateTile.tsx:47 derives the pill from setpoint via modeFromTarget() instead of data.action. Surface data.action in the mode pill.
3. Extra icon box: ClimateTile.tsx:85-87 wraps the thermo icon in <span className=ic 26x26>. Design Sec (evee-tiles.jsx:5-13) renders the icon bare at s=19. Use the shared TileHeader/Sec primitive.
acceptance_criteria: HA outage: shimmer skeleton, no 70°F / 72° ambient. No FALLBACK constants in tile or service; mutation doesn't hardcode ambient. Mode pill reflects live data.action. Header uses shared TileHeader (bare icon s=19, no ic box).

title: ControlsTile: remove fake FALLBACK + shimmer, relabel 'More'→'Scene', spin fan when on
description:
apps/web/src/components/tiles/ControlsTile.tsx + apps/api/src/services/controls-service.ts.
1. Fake FALLBACK (P0): ControlsTile.tsx:20-24 { lamps:{on:true,count:2,sub:'2 on · warm'}, ... } and :87 data ?? FALLBACK — shows 'Lamps ON · 2 on · warm' on load/outage. API FALLBACK at controls-service.ts:34-38,115-130. Service throws; tile shows shimmer when !data.
2. Optimistic toggle corrupts empty cache: ControlsTile.tsx:95-119 onMutate uses old ?? FALLBACK; if toggled before first query it writes fake values and onError restores fake state. Guard: if (!old) return — block mutation before initial data loads.
3. 4th cell label 'More' → design shows 'Scene' (Evee Dashboard.html:93, dash-r8.png, evee4.png). ControlsTile.tsx:212.
4. Fan icon doesn't spin when on: ControlsTile.tsx:57. Reference evee/apps/web/src/components/tiles/fan/fan-tile-view.tsx:16-19 (CSS spin, animationPlayState running/paused by fan.on). Add spin.
Build on shared primitives/Skeleton.
acceptance_criteria: HA outage: shimmer skeleton, no 'Lamps ON · 2 on · warm'. No FALLBACK constants. 4th button labeled 'Scene'. Fan icon spins when fan.on, paused when off. Toggles don't fire before initial data loads.

title: TeslaTile: remove fake fallback, add tile chrome/padding, fix header title order
description:
apps/web/src/components/tiles/TeslaTile.tsx + apps/api/src/services/tesla-service.ts.
1. Fake fallback (P0): TeslaTile.tsx:5-18 FALLBACK { pct:82, rate:25, range:264, climate:70, charging:true } and :275 data ?? FALLBACK — shows 82%/264mi as if real. Service TESLA_PLACEHOLDER returned at tesla-service.ts:71,84 and trpc/routers/tesla.ts:24-29. Service must throw; tile shows shimmer when !data. (NOTE: odometer already fixed by Calum — do not reintroduce or re-touch odometer.)
2. Missing card chrome: TeslaTile.tsx:279 root div has no className=tile (border/bg/radius/shadow). Design Evee Dashboard.html:71 wraps Tesla in <div className=tile>. Add it — or better, use the shared Tile primitive.
3. Missing padding: add padding 22 (design Evee Dashboard.html:71).
4. Header title order: approved screenshot evee4.png shows primary heading 'Evee' (nickname) + 'MODEL Y' badge, subtitle 'Tesla · Home'. CC TeslaTile.tsx:58-84 has 'Tesla' large with nickname as subtitle — swapped.
5. refetchInterval inline 60_000 at TeslaTile.tsx:271 → use POLL.tesla (lib/hooks.ts:23).
6. Dead className=ic on car chip TeslaTile.tsx:33 — remove.
acceptance_criteria: HA outage: shimmer skeleton, zero fake numbers. No FALLBACK/TESLA_PLACEHOLDER constants. Tile has card chrome (className=tile or Tile primitive) + padding 22. Primary heading = nickname ('Evee'), subtitle = 'Tesla · [place]'. refetchInterval uses POLL.tesla. Dead ic className removed. Odometer behavior left as Calum fixed it.

title: NetworkTile: remove all fake data (GB totals, ping, sine-wave chart, false Online) + match design
description:
NetworkTile is the worst fake-data offender and also doesn't match the design WiFi tile.
1. Hardcoded GB totals: network-service.ts:44-45 FALLBACK_DOWN_GB=14.2 / FALLBACK_UP_GB=3.8, rendered as real at NetworkTile.tsx:69-70.
2. Bogus 24h totals from one bps snapshot: integrations/unifi/index.ts:111-113 (txRateBps*86400*0.001) — use the real UniFi hourly stats endpoint; UnifiHourlyBucket (index.ts:33-40) is defined but never used.
3. Sine-wave butterfly chart is ALWAYS fake: network-service.ts:33-38 generateFallbackTraffic() set at line 114 even when UniFi is reachable; duplicated on the client NetworkTile.tsx:5-8 FALLBACK_TRAFFIC. No code path ever returns real buckets.
4. Hardcoded ping: network-service.ts:41 FALLBACK_PING_MS=12 → footer shows 12ms.
5. False Online on outage: network-service.ts:116-122 makeFallback(ssid,'Online'); client NetworkTile.tsx:67 status ?? 'Online'.
6. SSID from env not UniFi: network-service.ts:87 env.WIFI_SSID || 'Home'.
7. SSID shown twice: NetworkTile.tsx:147-149 and :179 — design (evee-tiles.jsx:364) shows it only in the footer.
Fix: remove all fallback constants; service throws on UniFi failure; wire real hourly buckets + real ping + live SSID; show shimmer skeleton on outage; SSID only in footer. Build on the shared Skeleton + primitives.
acceptance_criteria: UniFi up: real measured GB totals (not bps-extrapolated), real ping, real hourly buckets, live SSID. UniFi down: shimmer skeleton, no 'Online', no 14.2/3.8 GB, no 12ms, no sine chart. No FALLBACK_* or generateFallbackTraffic in the codebase. SSID only in footer.

title: No fake data: first-load + outage show shimmer skeleton and retry, never invented values
description:
Hard rule (Calum): tiles must NEVER show fake/hardcoded/placeholder values — not on first load, not on outage. On both INITIAL load and any downstream API failure a tile shows a shimmering skeleton and keeps retrying until real data arrives. Aggressive refresh is fine (LAN).

API layer — services swallow errors and return hardcoded constants, so tRPC returns 200 with fake data and the client can't detect an outage. Remove these catch-returns; let errors propagate:
- apps/api/src/services/weather-service.ts:139-141 (PLACEHOLDER_NOW), :168-171 (PLACEHOLDER_HOURLY)
- apps/api/src/services/tesla-service.ts:71,84 (TESLA_PLACEHOLDER)
- apps/api/src/services/controls-service.ts:115-130 (FALLBACK)
- apps/api/src/services/climate-service.ts:33,59 (FALLBACK)
- apps/api/src/services/network-service.ts:116-130 (makeFallback)
- apps/api/src/services/events-service.ts:63-66 (PLACEHOLDER_EVENTS)

Frontend layer — tiles do data ?? FALLBACK, rendering fake numbers on first mount AND on error:
- ControlsTile.tsx:87, Next12Hours.tsx:45, EventsTile.tsx:104, TeslaTile.tsx:275, ClimateTile.tsx:36, NetworkTile.tsx:67-74.

Fix plan:
1. Each API service throws on error/unconfigured instead of returning fake data.
2. Configure QueryClient in apps/web/src/lib/trpc.ts: defaultOptions.queries = { retry: true, retryDelay: ~5000, refetchOnWindowFocus: false }. Infinite retry for the wall panel.
3. Every tile: if (!data) return <tile skeleton>  (covers first load AND error). No data ?? FALLBACK.
4. Delete ALL FALLBACK/PLACEHOLDER constant blocks (api + web).
5. Use the shared Skeleton primitive for every tile's loading layout.

Reference: evee/apps/web/src/hooks/use-auto-reload.ts, evee/apps/web/src/components/placeholder-tile.tsx. Per-tile follow-ups are tracked separately and depend on this + the primitives epic.
acceptance_criteria: On first load every tile shows an animated shimmer skeleton until real data arrives (no fake values flash in). On any outage (HA/UniFi/weather/DB down) every affected tile reverts to shimmer skeleton and keeps retrying. No FALLBACK/PLACEHOLDER constants anywhere (api or web). API services throw on error. Manual test: disconnect HA and reload — zero invented numbers visible.

title: Controls anti-flicker: server-side desired-state window + 1s reconcile poll (port from evee, NO websocket)
description:
PROBLEM: when a user taps a control (lights/lamps/fan), Home Assistant does NOT immediately report the new state. control-center today does frontend-only optimism (ControlsTile.tsx:89-134 React-Query onMutate flip + onSettled invalidate) over a stateless controls-service.ts. The next poll re-reads stale HA state and the tile FLICKERS back. Per-client only, defeated by the next refetch.

evee already solved this and we MUST copy the exact same approach here. NO WEBSOCKETS — evee's anti-flicker is a DB-backed desired-state window reconciled by a 1-second POLL loop. (evee has a ws-relay.ts but it is NOT part of this mechanism; do not introduce websockets.)

EXACT evee mechanism (verified, quote file:line):
1) DB columns on the device/control state row (evee db/schema.ts:178-207 deviceState): reportedState/reportedAtUtc/reportedChangedAtUtc (HA truth) + desiredState/desiredAtUtc/desiredUntilUtc (the user's intent + window). Plus a deviceCommands audit table (evee schema.ts:209+): status pending|sent|confirmed|failed|timeout, issued/sent/confirmed timestamps, error.
2) Command write (evee device-command-service.ts:10,27-79): DESIRED_WINDOW_MS = 5_000. On command: set desiredState + desiredUntilUtc = now+5s (extend window on rapid re-toggle, last-write-wins, lines 34-37); insert deviceCommands row status 'pending'; dispatch to HA through a PER-DEVICE serial queue (PER_DEVICE_QUEUE, lines 11,148-158) so commands to the same device don't race; mark 'sent' after the HA call, 'failed' on error.
3) Merged read (evee device-service.ts:46-51 effective()): if desiredUntilUtc > now and desiredState set -> return desiredState; else return reportedState. The dashboard reads this MERGED value, so during the 5s window it shows the user's intent, never stale HA.
4) Reconcile loop (evee device-sync-service.ts:12,19-98): a self-scheduling setTimeout loop at SYNC_INTERVAL_MS = 1_000 (1s). Each tick: fetch HA snapshot for light+switch domains; update reportedState only when changed; if reported == desired and window still open -> clear the window EARLY and mark the latest sent command 'confirmed'; then sweepExpiredWindows() clears windows past their TTL and marks the command 'timeout' if HA never agreed (so a failed command doesn't lie forever).
5) Mapping (evee device-state-mapping.ts): mapHaToReported(kind,entity) + stateEquals() for change detection.
6) Frontend (evee lights-tile.tsx): NO onMutate optimism; poll device.list every 1s and render server effective-state. Mutation response already returns the device with the window active so it updates instantly.

PORT into control-center:
- apps/api: add desired/reported columns to the controls store (or a new deviceState-style table) + a deviceCommands audit table; drizzle migration.
- apps/api: device-command-service equivalent (write desired window + per-device queue dispatch to HA via existing integrations/homeassistant).
- apps/api: device-sync-service equivalent — 1s setTimeout poll loop doing reconcile + early-confirm + sweepExpiredWindows. Start it from the api entrypoint (apps/api/src/index.ts / server.ts). NO websocket.
- apps/api: effective()/merge helper + expose via trpc: controls.list returns merged state with a 'pending'/optimistic flag; controls.toggle (and climate setpoint / fan speed) writes the window then returns merged state.
- apps/web: ControlsTile drops the onMutate cache hack; polls ~1s and renders server merged state (poll faster while pending, slower when idle). Optional subtle pending hint.
- Apply the same desired-window to ClimateTile setpoint and fan speed (non-boolean desired values).

Reference files in evee (apps/api/src): services/device-command-service.ts, services/device-sync-service.ts, services/device-service.ts, services/device-state-mapping.ts, db/schema.ts (deviceState + deviceCommands); apps/web/src/components/tiles/lights/lights-tile.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: Tapping a control updates the tile instantly and does NOT flicker back while HA catches up. Desired state is held SERVER-SIDE in the DB (survives refetch, consistent across multiple dashboard clients), confirmed early when the 1s reconcile poll sees HA match, and snaps back to HA truth (command marked timeout) if unconfirmed after the 5s window. Implemented with a polling reconcile loop — NO websockets anywhere. controls.list/toggle (+ climate setpoint, fan speed) return merged state with a pending flag. ControlsTile no longer relies on React-Query onMutate for correctness. No fake/placeholder data (outage still shows shimmer per www-nra).

title: Consistency gate: every tile uses shared primitives, one spacing/type scale, identical loading/error, zero fake data
description:
Final production-readiness pass once primitives + per-tile work land. Make the board consistent and polished as a whole, not tile-by-tile.
Checklist:
- Every tile uses the shared Tile + TileHeader + Stat/Pill/Chip/StatusDot/Skeleton primitives; no tile keeps an inline section header or bespoke card shell.
- One spacing scale and one type scale across all tiles (header sizes, paddings, gaps from tokens/props, not magic numbers).
- Identical loading/error/outage treatment everywhere (shared Skeleton; first-load + outage both shimmer; no per-tile snowflake states).
- Zero fake/placeholder/FALLBACK constants anywhere (api + web) — grep clean.
- Icons/colors/borders/radii consistent with tokens.css + evee.css.
- typecheck + lint + tests pass; visually verified against design screenshots.
Depends on the primitives epic and all per-tile issues.
acceptance_criteria: grep for FALLBACK/PLACEHOLDER/mock returns nothing in apps/. No tile defines its own section header or card shell. Spacing/type come from shared primitives/tokens. All tiles shimmer identically on first load + outage. typecheck+lint+tests green. Board matches design screenshots.

title: WeatherNow: real shimmer animation; show skeleton on error instead of dash layout
description:
apps/web/src/components/tiles/WeatherNow.tsx.
1. Static skeleton: SkeletonLine (lines 58-70) is a static div (background var(--tile-2), opacity .6) with no animation — not 'shimmering'. Replace with the shared animated Skeleton primitive.
2. Error shows dim dash layout: on isError (lines 78-92) the tile renders the full layout with '--' at .55 opacity instead of a skeleton. On isError (not just isLoading) render the shimmer skeleton. Pairs with the global fix making weather-service.ts:139-141 throw.
Reference evee/apps/web/src/components/placeholder-tile.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: Initial load AND any weather error: WeatherNow shows animated shimmer skeleton (gradient sweep). No '--' dash layout on error. No static grey rectangle skeletons.

title: Design system: shared tile primitives (Tile, TileHeader, Stat, Pill, Chip, StatusDot, Skeleton)
description:
Every tile hand-rolls the same primitives, so spacing/typography/behavior drift between tiles. Build them ONCE so the whole board is consistent and production-grade.

Current duplication:
- Section header rebuilt inline in ControlsTile.tsx:151-168 and NetworkTile.tsx:88-127 (fontSize 17.5/weight 600/letterSpacing -.015em), a THIRD variant in TilePlaceholder.tsx (.sec/.ic/.lbl), and the design's <Sec icon right> in /private/tmp/design-bundle/evee/project/evee-tiles.jsx:5-13. Three headers, none shared.
- Tile shell: every tile repeats className=tile + height:100%/flex/column/padding.
- Stat block (design <Stat label value accent sub> evee-tiles.jsx:16-24), mode Chip, Pill, live StatusDot — all inlined per tile.

Build under apps/web/src/components/ui/ (or components/primitives/):
- Tile: card shell (className=tile, height:100%, flex column, configurable padding prop) so per-tile padding (clock 28, weath 24, ctrl/net 22, events 18x26) is a prop not a copy.
- TileHeader (port of design Sec): props { icon, title, right?, iconSize=19, titleSize=17.5 } — must cover BOTH the small WiFi/Controls header AND the larger Tesla header by varying size props.
- Stat { label, value, accent?, sub? }
- Pill { tone: default|on|amber } and Chip { active }
- StatusDot (the pulsing .dot)
- Skeleton: reusable shimmer block with a @keyframes shimmer gradient-sweep (added to styles/globals.css). This is the building block for ALL loading/outage states. TilePlaceholder.tsx is only a static icon scaffold — replace it.

Reference good componentization: evee/apps/web/src/components/tile.tsx. Design tokens already in styles/tokens.css + evee.css.

This is the foundation epic: the per-tile fidelity/outage issues depend on it.
acceptance_criteria: Primitives exist under apps/web/src/components/ui/. TileHeader/Tile/Stat/Pill/Chip/StatusDot/Skeleton are exported and unit-rendered. Size variation is via props (one TileHeader serves both small and large headers). A @keyframes shimmer exists in globals.css and Skeleton uses it. No tile defines its own inline section header after adoption (tracked by the consistency-gate issue). No fake/placeholder data introduced.

title: Next12Hours: make the 'Feels' line more subtle
description:
The dashed 'Feels like' polyline in the Next 12 Hours chart is too prominent and competes with the temperature bars. Make it more subtle.
File: apps/web/src/components/tiles/Next12Hours.tsx (the feels-like polyline + dots, mirrors design evee-tiles.jsx:393-394 EHourlyBF).
Currently stroke #6E747D, strokeWidth ~1.6, strokeDasharray '2 5', plus r=1.7 dots at each point.
Options to dial back: lower opacity (e.g. ~0.4-0.5), thinner stroke (~1), drop or shrink the per-point dots, and/or a dimmer grey. Keep it readable as a secondary reference under the temp bars. Match against the design but err on the side of subtle per Calum.
acceptance_criteria: The feels-like line reads clearly as a secondary/subtle element beneath the temperature bars (reduced opacity/weight, less prominent dots); temp bars remain the visual focus. No fake data added.

1. No section header: DogCamTile.tsx:43-47 goes straight to the feed shell button. Design line 83 renders <Sec icon='cam'>Dog Cam</Sec> above <EDogCam/>. It is the only tile missing a header. Add the shared TileHeader (icon 'cam', title 'Dog Cam').
2. Padding: DogCamTile.tsx:46 has padding 16; design line 82 specifies padding 20. Change 16→20.
acceptance_criteria: DogCamTile has a top header row with camera icon + 'Dog Cam' via the shared TileHeader; inner padding is 20 (matching Evee Dashboard.html line 82); consistent with other tiles' headers.

title: iPad PWA: apply safe-area-inset so the board clears the home bar
description: index.html:7,10 sets viewport-fit=cover + apple-mobile-web-app-capable=yes, so in PWA mode on calums-ipad-pro the bottom of the 1366x1024 board overlaps the home indicator. Neither styles/globals.css:11-19 nor Board.tsx applies env(safe-area-inset-bottom). Add padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom) to body or #stage. Production-only; not modelled in the desktop prototype.
acceptance_criteria: On the iPad Pro wall panel in PWA mode no tile is obscured by the home bar; env(safe-area-inset-bottom) applied at body/#stage.

title: ClockGreeting: AM/PM letters look squished (inherited negative tracking)
description: In ClockGreeting.tsx:54-65 the time wrapper is .mono with letterSpacing:-.05em. The AM/PM span (line 64) only sets fontSize:26 + marginLeft:8 and INHERITS the -.05em, so at 26px the two caps are pulled together and read as squished. marginLeft separates AM/PM from the time but nothing fixes spacing between the A and M. Fix: set letterSpacing:'0.02em' (or 'normal') on the AM/PM span. NOTE the design (evee-tiles.jsx EClockGreeting) has the same inherited-tracking issue, so this is a genuine fix not a port error.
acceptance_criteria: AM/PM renders with normal/positive tracking, legibly spaced, distinct from the time digits. No regression to the 96px time tracking.

title: Weather Now: swap Sunset/Sunrise metric cell based on time of day
description:
In the Weather Now card, the bottom metric cell currently always shows 'Sunset' with the sunset time. Once it's past sunset for the day, that value is stale/less useful. Calum wants the cell to flip to the *next* relevant event: show 'Sunset' + sunset time while before sunset, then after sunset passes show 'Sunrise' + the next sunrise time. It should keep swapping between the two across the day so it always shows the upcoming event.

Example: sunset 7:58 PM. Before 7:58 PM show 'Sunset 7:58 PM'. At/after 7:58 PM show 'Sunrise 4:14 AM' (next morning's sunrise). After that sunrise passes, swap back to showing the day's sunset.
design:
Current state:
- apps/api/src/services/weather-service.ts only requests 'sunset' from Open-Meteo daily (line ~131) and formats it via formatSunset() (line ~113). No sunrise is fetched.
- apps/api/src/trpc/routers/weather.ts schema exposes 'sunset: string' (line 13).
- apps/web/src/components/tiles/WeatherNow.tsx renders <MetricCell label='Sunset' value={w.sunset} /> (line ~180), default sunset '--:--' (line 13).

Work needed:
1. API: add 'sunrise' to the Open-Meteo daily query, parse + format it (reuse/rename formatSunset), and include sunrise in the WeatherData type + tRPC output schema. Likely want today's sunrise/sunset AND tomorrow's sunrise so the 'next event' can be computed when past today's sunset.
2. Web: compute which event is next based on current time vs sunset/sunrise, and render label+value accordingly (Sunset before sunset, Sunrise after). Decide whether the swap logic lives client-side (needs the raw times, recompute on a timer/render) or server-side (API returns the already-resolved next event).
3. Update WeatherNow.test.tsx and weather.test.ts for both states.

Open question: pure client-side computation is simplest for the live swap, but requires the API to return raw sunrise + sunset (and tomorrow's sunrise). Flag for decision before implementing.

title: Tesla odometer shows placeholder (24,113), not real value
description:
The Tesla tile odometer shows a fixed 24,113 instead of the car's real mileage.

Actual root cause (the web tile + API wiring are correct — verified TeslaTile.tsx:270-275 consumes live tRPC data and tesla-service.ts:47 resolves sensor.evee_odometer):
- tesla-service.ts:97-100 comments 'Odometer is frequently disabled in the integration — fall back gracefully' and returns TESLA_PLACEHOLDER.odo = '24,113' whenever the odometer entity is missing/unavailable.
- So the displayed 24,113 is the design placeholder, surfacing because sensor.evee_odometer is not returning a real value from Home Assistant (entity disabled in the Tesla integration, or asleep).

Note: user recalled the number as 47821; the actual hardcoded placeholder is 24,113.

Fix options:
- Enable the odometer sensor entity in the Tesla (tesla_custom / Tesla Fleet) HA integration so sensor.evee_odometer reports a value, then the live value flows through automatically.
- If the entity genuinely can't be enabled, source odometer another way, or hide the stat rather than showing a misleading static number.
close_reason: Code described in issue (discarded query) was already fixed in ffc593d. Real cause of live 24,113: sensor.evee_odometer was disabled-by-default in the Tesla HA integration, so the API correctly fell back. Enabled the entity via HA websocket entity_registry API + woke the car (button.evee_wake) -> odometer now reports 38,743 live through tesla.get. Also fixed a surfaced bug: odometer rendered '0' (not placeholder) when entity exists but state is unknown/unavailable; now degrades to placeholder + regression test added.

title: Add favicon using the evee logo
description:
apps/web/index.html (lines 1-18) has no <link rel=icon> and there is no apps/web/public/ favicon. Use the evee logo per Calum.
1. Create apps/web/public/.
2. Copy /Users/calum/code/github.com/0x63616c/evee/apps/web/public/logo.png to apps/web/public/favicon.png (evee logo also at evee/logo.png).
3. Add <link rel='icon' type='image/png' href='/favicon.png' /> in index.html after the charset meta.
acceptance_criteria: Browser tab shows the evee logo. apps/web/public/favicon.png exists. <link rel=icon> present in index.html.
